blocks.body: Personal pronouns are the most important pronouns in the English language. They have two forms: subject pronouns to denote the one who performs the action, and object pronouns to denote the one who receives the action.

{ "title": "7. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Subject pronouns - before the verb (who acts): I, you, he, she, it, we, they.", "Object pronouns — after a verb/preposition (who/what): me, you, him, her, it, us, them.", "I is always capitalized, even within a sentence.", "You and it — the same form for subject and object.", "After prepositions (with, for, to, about) always object pronouns.", "In short answers, usually object: Who did it? — Me (not I).", "He/she/it — for people and living things. It is for objects, animals, weather.", "Check yourself: Who? → subject. Who/To whom? → object." ] }
